title: triticum spontaneum var. pseudobaeticum
intro: Triticum spontaneum var. pseudobaeticum, also known as a wild wheat or spelt, is a subspecies of wild wheat native to the mountainous regions of northern Spain and southern France. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and essential oils. The health benefits of Triticum spontaneum var. pseudobaeticum include its ability to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and act as an antiseptic agent to prevent infections. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
